authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 518210 Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides the official industry classification boundary for data-center and cloud-hosting operations, preventing generic handling from being used as a substitute.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service taxonomy, industry readiness scope, and terminology binding

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-145, The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines cloud service and deployment terminology that determines what support artifacts and boundaries are material for this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-146, Cloud Computing Synopsis and Recommendations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides recognized operational considerations for cloud service operation, availability, and reliability behavior used in dependency and readiness routing.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines control domains for access, configuration, logging, and response that inform evidence sufficiency and escalation in support cases.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61r2, Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Supplies standard incident handling and containment guidance directly relevant to support/incident packaging and ownership routing.

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud Controls Matrix (v4.0.6)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides practical control domains and control-to-evidence expectations used by cloud service professionals for control-sensitive workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1, Information Technology Service Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides established service-management expectations for incident, change, and handoff records that directly shape required output artifacts.

workflow_stages.name: Support intake and scope verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate request intent, tenant ownership, and minimum evidence before any actionable recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect and map tickets, access artifacts, change-readiness evidence, and integration dependencies to determine safe next actions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Resolution decision and communication
workflow_stages.description: Produce resolution, communication, and escalation shapes in a bounded format with unresolved items surfaced.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and authority handoff
workflow_stages.description: Route cases that require adjacent-specialist authority, policy exceptions, or unresolved tenant-policy conflicts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant adaptation and repeat-failure management
workflow_stages.description: Handle policy and terminology differences through explicit assumptions, repeat-failure escalation, and no-confident completion when conflicts persist.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Change requests, change logs, and rollback plans

allowed: Validate scope and evidence before recommending action.
Return escalation-oriented communication and handoffs for unresolved authority cases.
Produce evidence-aware service resolution when all required gates are satisfied.
escalate: Critical interface dependency failure or ownership ambiguity
Conflicting tenant policy and public-source requirements
refuse: Unsupported policy exception or discretionary workaround requests
Requests requiring final legal, financial, engineering, or licensing authority
Resolution attempts without change-readiness or approval evidence

domain_failure_modes: Applying general customer support phrases without mapping cloud/data-center artifacts like tenancy, zone, dependency, and change windows.
Marking request complete while required evidence or approvals are still missing.
Routing to wrong adjacent owner when access or dependency authority is not explicitly verified.
Ignoring repeated-failure patterns and repeating guidance without increased escalation.
Overriding tenant-specific security or change policy due to generic cloud guidance assumptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific definitions for change lock and maintenance window authority
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different tenants may define the same terms with stricter or looser escalation semantics.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require explicit tenant term map and ownership declarations before resolving in-scope cases.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Required evidence depth for access and rollback in high-complexity multi-tenant incidents
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Evidence packages can vary materially by tenant criticality class.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat evidence depth as tenant-provided policy input and escalate when absent.

notes: Public cloud and service-management references are combined with industry classification to make lane outputs materially different from generic support workflows.
Workflow stages cover service intake, evidence review, resolution, escalation, and adaptation handling with explicit boundary outcomes.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity is left; each ambiguity is converted into required tenant context and an escalation condition.
